findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new API consists of `corestrict_obj_*`, `corestrict_map_*`, inclusion/forgetful pointwise lemmas, and only `corestrict_map_comp_coalg_toLinearMap`/`corestrict_map_comp_coalg_apply` at lines 166 and 176; there is no declaration for functor identity or composed corestriction.
findings.fix: Add characteristic natural isomorphisms or equalities such as `corestrictIdIso : corestrict (CoalgHom.id R C) ≅ 𝟭 _` and `corestrictCompIso (f) (g) : corestrict (g.comp f) ≅ corestrict f ⋙ corestrict g`, with `@[simp]` lemmas for their components and underlying maps.
findings.issue: `FGComoduleCat.corestrict` has no functor-level API for `CoalgHom.id` or composition of coalgebra morphisms; lines 166-181 only compare underlying maps/elements, leaving users without a named way to identify `corestrict (g.comp f)` with `corestrict f ⋙ corestrict g` on objects.

summary: The new finite corestriction functor exposes only pointwise map-level composition, so the public API does not characterize the functor under identity and composition of coalgebra morphisms.
